items.title: Secure area with one exit left hospital staff trapped by armed patient

children.content: No-one was injured during the incident at Canberra Hospital but staff were stuck inside a "secure staff station" for three hours while police disarmed a man with a knife.

image.altText: A sign that says "Emergency Building 5'' and "Main entry'' pointing to a building.

items.title: How beer and AI are changing the course of Canberra's economic future

children.content: The capital has long been known as a public service town, but with about two in three workers now employed in the private sector and the population expected to reach nearly 700,000 by 2050, is that reputation — and key economic driver — likely to change?

image.altText: A man stands and smiles, leaning on a bench in a large workshop space with buckets and gear behind him.

items.title: 'Self-belief, respect': Indigenous boxers building strength with David Pocock

children.content: A Canberra Indigenous-led boxing camp is helping young fighters hone their skills for life inside and outside the ring in its first development camp, continuing a proud tradition of First Nations involvement in the sport.

gallery.altText: A young boy hits a punching bag in a boxing gym.

items.title: 'Carry the hope': Thousands gather for Anzac Day service in Canberra

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and New Zealand High Commissioner Andrew Needs are among those to gather in Canberra for the 111th anniversary of the Gallipoli landings.

image.altText: The facade of the Australian War Memorial was lit up before dawn on Anzac Day 2026.

items.title: 'Not what the community wanted': Big Splash lease spared despite backlash

children.content: ACT planning authorities have decided they will not follow through with a threat to terminate the lease of Big Splash water park, saying they are now satisfied the operators have made moves to reopen later this year.

image.altText: Three tower waterslides, all in a significant state of disrepair, surrounded by gum trees.

items.title: Want to buy a football team? Why this club's next chapter is unwritten

children.content: Canberra United is once again without an owner and facing an uncertain future, in a tale all too familiar for players and fans of the A-League's only stand-alone women's team.

items.title: Coalition points to stealth bombers as potential AUKUS stop-gap

children.content: The Coalition says Australia should consider buying next-generation stealth bombers to help bridge the gap until the AUKUS submarines arrive.

items.title: Affidavit reveals more details as man charged with attempted Trump assassination

items.title: Police honour Kalgoorlie detectives brutally killed 100 years ago

children.content: On this day in 1926, Detective Inspector John Walsh and Detective Sergeant Alexander Pitman were shot while trying to apprehend gold thieves near Kalgoorlie. Their charred and dismembered bodies were later found at the bottom of a mine shaft.
